name: Case Studies 1

keyElements: Section heading with subtitle and 'View All Case Studies' button
Numbered tabs (01–04) for case study navigation
Case study title, description, and image placeholder
Three large percentage statistics with supporting text
'View Case Study' CTA button
Vertical side tabs with rotated labels

SaaS companies with measurable client outcomes
Professional service providers (marketing, IT, management)
chooseWhen: You have 3–5 high-impact case studies with strong quantitative results
Storytelling and proof of ROI are critical to the sales cycle
You want to showcase each case study in depth rather than as a brief overview
avoidWhen: You have 10+ case studies that need equal visibility
Results are qualitative rather than metric-based
Users need to filter or search case studies by industry or service type

Three statistics per card with labels
'Case Study' link/button per card

Businesses with recognizable client logos that add credibility

You want to pair social proof with an immediate contact option like a phone number CTA
avoidWhen: You lack recognizable client logos (empty slots look weak)
Case studies don't have clean quantitative metrics
Your audience prefers deep narrative over quick stats
conversion: Generate direct inquiries (phone call or CTA click) by demonstrating breadth of successful engagements
assetsRequired: Client logos (high-res SVG/PNG), sub-service labels, three quantitative stats per case study with labels, section headline, CTA text and phone number, full case study page links

SaaS platforms and marketing companies using visual storytelling
Businesses in creative, tech, or marketing industries

name: Blog 1
layout: A vertically stacked blog listing page featuring a hero header with breadcrumbs, a prominent featured post, a filterable 3-column article grid with pagination, a newsletter signup section, and a full-width CTA banner at the bottom.

Featured post card (large split-layout with image left, title/excerpt right)
Category filter pills (horizontal tag-based filtering with 'Clear Filters' action)
Blog post cards (3-column grid with thumbnail, category label, title, excerpt, and read time)
Pagination controls (numbered pages with previous/next arrows)
Email newsletter signup (input field + 'Sign Up' button with terms disclaimer)
bestFor: Content marketing agencies or companies with active blogging strategies
SaaS/tech companies publishing thought leadership and educational content
Media outlets or online magazines with regular editorial output
Professional services firms (law, consulting, healthcare) using content for SEO authority
chooseWhen: The client publishes blog content regularly (weekly or more) and has a growing library
Multiple content categories exist and users need filtering to find relevant topics
SEO and organic traffic acquisition are primary marketing objectives
Building an email subscriber list is a parallel business goal
avoidWhen: Sparse content library — fewer than ~9 posts will make the grid look empty and pagination unnecessary
Visually-driven brands (photography, design portfolios) where this text-heavy card format underserves the imagery
Single-topic businesses where category filtering adds unnecessary complexity

assetsRequired: Blog post content (titles, body copy, excerpts, category assignments); featured/thumbnail images for each post (consistent aspect ratio); defined category taxonomy; a featured/pinned post selection for the hero area; CTA banner copy and phone number; newsletter terms & conditions / privacy policy link
